title: LEFT / RIGHT / MID
summary: LEFT, RIGHT, and MID extract portions of text from a string. LEFT pulls characters from the start, RIGHT from the end, and MID from any position in between.
jobUseCases: Extracting a region code from the first two characters of a product ID
Pulling the last four digits of an account number for masking
Splitting a fixed-format employee code into department and serial parts
Getting the file extension or prefix from a list of document names
Separating area codes from phone numbers stored in a single column

examples.title: LEFT to get a prefix
examples.explanation: Returns 'IN', the first 2 characters of the string. LEFT counts from the start of the text for the number of characters you specify.

examples.title: RIGHT to get a suffix
examples.explanation: Returns the last 4 characters of the value in A2, for example the serial '0481' from an order code or the last 4 digits of an account number.

examples.title: MID to extract the middle
examples.explanation: Starts at position 4 and pulls 4 characters, returning '2026'. MID is ideal for grabbing a year or segment from a fixed-format code.

examples.title: MID with FIND for variable positions
examples.explanation: Uses FIND to locate the dash, then MID extracts the 4 characters after it. Combining with FIND or SEARCH handles strings where the delimiter position varies.

commonMistakes: Using 0 or a negative num_chars, which returns blank or a #VALUE! error
Off-by-one errors in MID; start_num is 1-based, so the first character is position 1, not 0
Expecting numeric results; these functions always return text, so wrap in VALUE to do math
Hardcoding positions when the format varies; use FIND or SEARCH to locate delimiters dynamically
Counting spaces or hidden characters by mistake, which shifts the extracted segment
practiceTips: Combine LEFT, RIGHT, and MID with FIND or SEARCH for delimiters that move around
Use LEN to calculate dynamic lengths, e.g. RIGHT(A2, LEN(A2)-FIND("-", A2))
For modern Excel, consider TEXTSPLIT or Text to Columns when splitting many fields at once
